summary: The dark-mode diff is strongly guide-aligned. Backend-guide: no business-logic surface added — the only Ruby is ThemeHelper#dark_theme?, a trivial presentation helper reading cookies[:theme] (correctly a helper, not a service); no controllers/models/callbacks/jobs touched; Minitest used, with a helper unit test plus a system test covering the toggle/persist flow. UI-style-guide §Color is followed closely: neutrals are centralized as CSS custom-property tokens (bg-app/bg-surface/text-default/border-default/text-muted/text-subtle/bg-nav-active) with a class-based @custom-variant dark, and views were converted to those utilities; StatusHelper::TONE_CLASSES remains the single source of truth for status colors and uses exactly the guide's soft-badge + dark pattern, with the status word always rendered (§Accessibility). The icon-only toggle carries aria-label + title + aria-pressed. The guide itself was updated in the same PR (CLAUDE.md mandate). Two minor deviations remain (see findings); neither is blocking. Note on the prior iteration: its claim of 'zero raw grays left in any view' was inaccurate (F1 below), and its focus-pattern finding overstated app-wide consistency since focus-visible:outline-* already pre-exists on main in phases/show.html.erb.

findings.issue: The neutral (`else`) flash branch on line 7 still uses raw neutral utilities `bg-gray-50 text-gray-700 ring-gray-600/20` (with hand-rolled `dark:` variants). This is the sole view left carrying raw neutrals, and this PR edited that exact line (it added the dark: variants), so the conversion opportunity was in-hand. It violates guides/ui-style-guide.md §Color as amended by this same PR: 'Never write raw bg-white/bg-gray-*/text-gray-*/border-gray-*/divide-gray-*/ring-gray-* in a view — use the semantic utilities.' The green/red branches legitimately use reserved status colors; only the neutral fallback deviates. Route it through a semantic utility (e.g. bg-surface-hover) or reuse StatusHelper's `muted` tone. Minor: functionally correct in both themes.

The toggle's focus indicator uses `focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-indigo-600` rather than the guide's canonically named `focus-visible:ring-2 ring-indigo-600` (§Accessibility baseline / §Buttons). This is a genuine but borderline deviation: a visible indigo focus indicator IS present (no a11y regression), and the outline pattern already pre-exists on main in phases/show.html.erb buttons, so the toggle matches an established in-repo pattern even though it differs from the guide's stated token. The codebase is itself inconsistent (login/form controls use `ring`, phase buttons use `outline`); aligning the toggle to `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-indigo-600` would match the guide's literal convention. Minor.
